pages.full_text:
given me nurofen, Simon has given me 2 glasses of Cremant de Bourgogne & it's wonderful to have no pain.
	Tinder is awful at pulling on the lead when we're near sheep.
	Topiaried hedges with peacocks. The land lady & husband (who died 2 years ago) have been there 50 years.
	A DoE Bronze group were there.

__Saturday~~ Sunday__
	Lovely sunny morning. We sat & chatted & read after breakfast. The DoE children formed a line & swept thro meadow looking for tith litter. After coffee we walked across the reservoir bridge. Then we had lunch & left. Tinder was behaving better by the end of the holiday.

__Saturday May 25th__ Peter's birthday.
11 Simon, Eve & Tinder left to go to Ansted so that Annette can chat with her executors, Kate (with baby Milo) & Eve. Kate, Annette & Eve meet on the Saturday. Pernille joined
pages.notes: Page continues from previous spread (starts mid-sentence). 'Saturday Sunday' heading appears to have 'Saturday' struck through or written over with 'Sunday' — transcribed as both with strikethrough marker. 'tith litter' may be [?tith] — unclear word, possibly 'tith' or 'litter' combined oddly; reads as 'tith litter' in context. 'Ansted' is the place name as written — could be Ansted/Anstead or a local village. Year 2013 confirmed: May 25 2013 was a Saturday, consistent with June 15 2013 also being a Saturday (see right page). 'executors' may relate to estate/probate context for Annette.

pages.full_text:
us on Sunday. We discussed Annette's care while Simon went on a bike ride. After Sunday lunch we head towards Richard Holroyd in Cambridge. He was not in. So we made a cup of tea & drove home.

__Saturday June 15th__
9.40. we leave for the south of England. We visit Standen, the Beale family home, a lovely Arts & Crafts house 1880. The interior is furnished as if from 1920. Mrs. Beale & 2 of her daughters sewed magnificent wall hangings, bedspreads, cushions & chair seats. In the dining room
pages.notes: Two glued-in photos: one showing a cosy library/sitting room interior with an armchair and bookshelves, one showing a garden with purple flowers (verbena-like) in front of a brick arch/window — likely photos of Standen. Text continues onto next page (ends mid-sentence 'In the dining room'). Richard Holroyd location given as Cambridge. Standen is a National Trust Arts & Crafts house in West Sussex, consistent with 'south of England'.
